# Pyarallel

pyarallel is a zero-dependency Python library for fanning out one function over many inputs against rate-limited services — parallel map with token-bucket rate limiting, retry with server-driven (Retry-After) backoff, SQLite checkpoint/resume, early abort, and constant-memory streaming, for sync and async.


## Getting Started

- [Home](https://oneryalcin.github.io/pyarallel/index.md): Overview and the fan-out niche
- [Quick Start](https://oneryalcin.github.io/pyarallel/getting-started/quickstart/index.md): Quick start
- [Which API Should I Use?](https://oneryalcin.github.io/pyarallel/getting-started/choosing-an-api/index.md): Choose collected, streaming, sync, or async
- [Comparison](https://oneryalcin.github.io/pyarallel/getting-started/comparison/index.md): When to use pyarallel vs alternatives
- [Troubleshooting](https://oneryalcin.github.io/pyarallel/user-guide/troubleshooting/index.md): Diagnose common failures
